name: Birmingham (United States)

colors.symbolism: the outer hoist and fly bands and the central star
the central band on which the seal is charged
the city's iron-and-steel heritage and the sun on the seal

description: Three vertical bands of red, white, and red (with the central white band wider than the outer red bands), charged at the centre with a large red five-pointed star and the gold seal of the City of Birmingham overlaid on the star.
history: Used by the City of Birmingham, the largest city in Alabama, founded in 1871 as an industrial centre during Reconstruction.

features.role: large red five-pointed star centred on the white band
Seal of the City of Birmingham
features.symbolism: The seal depicts the figure of Vulcan — the Roman god of the forge — alongside industrial elements (a hammer and anvil) symbolising Birmingham's foundation as an iron- and steel-producing city. Birmingham was named for its English industrial namesake and is dominated by the Vulcan statue erected for the 1904 World's Fair.
